questions.q: Indrajit was the eldest son of which demon king?
questions.explanation: Indrajit was the eldest son of Ravana, the ten-headed king of Lanka.

questions.q: What was Indrajit's birth name before he earned the title Indrajit?
questions.explanation: He was originally named Meghnad, said to be because he roared like thunder at birth.

questions.q: The title 'Indrajit' means the conqueror of whom?
questions.explanation: He earned the name Indrajit, meaning 'conqueror of Indra,' after defeating the king of the gods.

questions.q: Indrajit belonged to which race of beings?
questions.explanation: Indrajit was a rakshasa (demon), the son of the rakshasa king Ravana.

questions.q: Who was Indrajit's mother?
questions.explanation: Indrajit was the son of Ravana and his chief queen Mandodari.

questions.q: Indrajit was a master of which fighting art?
questions.explanation: Indrajit was renowned as a supreme master of maya, the art of magical illusion in warfare.

questions.q: In which kingdom did Indrajit live and fight?
questions.explanation: Indrajit was a prince of Lanka, the island kingdom ruled by his father Ravana.

questions.q: Which brother of Rama finally killed Indrajit?
questions.explanation: Indrajit was slain in battle by Lakshmana, Rama's devoted younger brother.

questions.q: Indrajit fought against the army of which prince of Ayodhya?
questions.explanation: Indrajit fought on his father Ravana's side against Rama's army during the war in Lanka.

questions.q: Indrajit was especially famous for using which type of weapon in battle?
questions.explanation: Indrajit was celebrated for his deadly arrows, including divine astras and the serpent-arrows.

questions.q: During the war, Indrajit bound Rama and Lakshmana using which weapon?
questions.explanation: Indrajit struck Rama and Lakshmana with the Naga-Pasha, snake-arrows that bound them like nooses.

questions.q: Who came to free Rama and Lakshmana from the serpent-bonds of Indrajit?
questions.explanation: Garuda, the divine eagle and enemy of serpents, arrived and the snakes fled, freeing the brothers.

questions.q: Indrajit was able to fight while hidden because of which power?
questions.explanation: A boon allowed Indrajit to fight while remaining invisible, making him nearly impossible to counter.

questions.q: From which deity had Indrajit performed worship to gain his great war-powers and chariot?
questions.explanation: Indrajit propitiated Agni and gained boons; his power was tied to fire-sacrifices and Brahma's gifts.

questions.q: To complete a special sacrifice for invincibility, Indrajit went to which temple in Lanka?
questions.explanation: Indrajit performed his secret yajna at the Nikumbhila shrine to make himself unconquerable.

questions.q: Who advised Rama that Indrajit must be slain before he completed his Nikumbhila sacrifice?
questions.explanation: Vibhishana, Ravana's righteous brother who joined Rama, revealed the secret of the Nikumbhila rite.

questions.q: On the first day Indrajit captured Indra, who eventually secured Indra's release?
questions.explanation: Brahma intervened, and in exchange for releasing Indra granted Meghnad the title Indrajit and boons.

questions.q: To break the vanaras' morale, Indrajit conjured an illusion of killing whom?
questions.explanation: Indrajit created a magical illusion of Sita and slew it before the army to crush their spirit.

questions.q: Which divine weapon did Indrajit unleash that struck down the entire vanara army at once?
questions.explanation: Indrajit released the Brahmastra, felling the vanara host including Rama and Lakshmana for a time.

questions.q: What was the name of Indrajit's chief wife?
questions.explanation: Indrajit's devoted wife was Sulochana, often described as the daughter of the serpent-king.

questions.q: Indrajit's banner on his chariot bore the emblem associated with which of his great feats?
questions.explanation: His pride and identity centered on his conquest of Indra, the feat that gave him his very name.

questions.q: During which book (kanda) of the Valmiki Ramayana does Indrajit's death occur?
questions.explanation: Indrajit's battles and death take place in the Yuddha Kanda, the book of the war in Lanka.

questions.q: Who guided Lakshmana to the Nikumbhila grove to confront Indrajit during his rite?
questions.explanation: Vibhishana led Lakshmana to Nikumbhila and pointed out Indrajit before his sacrifice could be finished.

questions.q: Which divine arrow did Lakshmana invoke to finally slay Indrajit?
questions.explanation: Lakshmana invoked the Aindrastra, the weapon of Indra himself, to behead and kill Indrajit.

questions.q: According to tradition, Lakshmana could kill Indrajit partly because of which personal austerity Lakshmana had kept?
options: He had fasted for fourteen years
He had not slept for fourteen years
He had taken a vow of silence
He had never wielded a bow before
questions.explanation: Tradition holds that Lakshmana, having gone without sleep for fourteen years, fulfilled the condition to slay the otherwise invincible Indrajit.

questions.q: What happened to Indrajit's wife Sulochana after his death, in popular tradition?
questions.explanation: In later tradition, the grieving Sulochana retrieved her husband's head and performed sati on his pyre.

questions.q: Indrajit defeated and bound Lakshmana once with a weapon that left him unconscious; who saved Lakshmana with healing?
questions.explanation: After Indrajit's Shakti/weapon felled Lakshmana, the vanara physician Sushena's healing herbs revived him.

questions.q: On how many separate days does Indrajit notably enter the battle in the Yuddha Kanda before his death?
options: On multiple days, returning after retreating
questions.explanation: Indrajit fought on more than one occasion, withdrawing into Lanka and re-emerging with fresh illusions and weapons.

questions.q: Which boon condition made Indrajit nearly unbeatable, requiring a special foe to kill him?
options: He could die only by a god's hand
He could be killed only by one who had conquered sleep and desire
He could die only at midnight
He could die only in water
questions.explanation: Tradition says Indrajit could be slain only by a warrior who had conquered sleep, food, and worldly comforts for years, a condition Lakshmana met.

questions.q: What was the immediate effect on Ravana when he learned of Indrajit's death?
options: He was overcome with grief and rage
questions.explanation: The death of his mightiest son plunged Ravana into overwhelming grief and fury, driving him to enter the war himself.
